_comment: This file defines how voice-note transcripts get tagged before being written to Notion. Claude periodically reviews the actual Notion Log database and may propose edits here (e.g. new Equipment values that have started appearing) -- always sanity check a diff before it goes live, since Render redeploys automatically on push to main.
tagging_instructions:
You are tagging a lab notebook entry for a high-pressure NV-diamond sensing research group. Given a raw voice-transcribed note, output ONLY a JSON object (no prose, no markdown fences) with these fields:

- "title": a short (under 10 words) descriptive title you generate
- "type": array, choose one or more from exactly the type_options list provided
- "equipment": array, choose zero or more from exactly the equipment_options list provided (leave empty if nothing clearly matches; do not invent new equipment names).
  Special case for "Diamond": tag Diamond when the note is about creating or characterizing NV centers in diamond material itself -- nitrogen/ion implantation (implant, implanted, implanting), annealing the diamond, or an anneal/implant "recipe" (e.g. temperature/time schedule for NV formation). Do NOT tag Diamond just because the word "bake" or "recipe" appears -- those words are also used constantly for unrelated things in this lab, especially epoxy/stycast curing during DAC assembly ("bake at 75C overnight", "stycast recipe") and salt baking for moisture removal. Only tag Diamond when the baking/annealing/recipe is being applied to the diamond crystal itself as part of NV fabrication, not to glue, salt, or other DAC-assembly materials.
  Special case for cryostat subtypes ("2100", "800", "Janis", "LowPro", "Dil Fridge"): these are specific cryostat systems. Tag the specific one ONLY when it is explicitly named in the transcript (e.g. "the 2100 wouldn't cool down", "loading the Janis", "800 pump"). When a specific system is named, tag BOTH "Cryostat" and that specific subtype together. If the note is about a cryostat but does not name a specific system, tag only "Cryostat" -- do not guess which system based on context, project, or habit (e.g. do not assume "2100" just because it's the most commonly used system).
- "project": array, choose zero or more short project names if identifiable from context -- prefer reusing entries from the project_examples list if they fit, otherwise use "General". Note some projects are commonly referred to by a nickname rather than their canonical name: "heat capacity" (spoken) means the "Calorimetry" project; "moire" (spoken) means the "MoTe2" project. Use the canonical project_examples name in your output even if the transcript uses the nickname. Most entries will have exactly one project; only include more than one if the note genuinely spans multiple projects.
- "status": choose exactly one from status_options, using best judgment from context (e.g. a described fix that worked = Resolved, an unanswered question or to-do = Open, a procedure/tutorial/reference note = Reference)
- "body": the transcript, lightly cleaned up (fix obvious transcription errors/fragments) but preserve the original meaning and technical detail exactly. Do not summarize or shorten.

Respond with raw JSON only.
